title: Plant Nuclear Genomic DNA Preparations
author: Elena Hilario
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rncd5aw

steps.title: Pour 300 mL ice-cold NEB complete buffer in a blender
steps.instruction: Pour 300 mL ice-cold NEB complete buffer in a blender. Work inside a fume hood.

steps.title: Weight 20 g of kiwifruit leaves,...
steps.instruction: Weight 20 g of kiwifruit leaves, cut any long stems and transfer to the blender

steps.title: Homogenize for 30 sec in low setting (Note
steps.instruction: Homogenize for 30 sec in low setting (Note: If your blender is powerful, use low setting, but if using a kitchen blender set it on High) Do not use a stick hand-held blender because the sample won't be homogenized all at the same time.

steps.title: Filtrate homogenate through 4 layers of...
steps.instruction: Filtrate homogenate through 4 layers of cheesecloth into a sterile glass cylinder It is best to use sterile glassware, but if you are confident your washing techniques are thorough, use non-sterile glassware. If you can't find good quality cheese cloth, you can use disposable kitchen cloth (for example CHUX superwipes )

steps.title: Filter again through 4 layers of...
steps.instruction: Filter again through 4 layers of miracloth into another 500 mL sterile glass cylinder Stack the miracloth layers against the fabric grain, i.e., perpendicular to each other

steps.title: Adjust the volume to 294 mL with NEB complete buffer
steps.instruction: Adjust the volume to 294 mL with NEB complete buffer

steps.title: Add 6 mL of 25% Triton...
steps.instruction: Add 6 mL of 25% Triton X-100 in NEB complete buffer by placing the tip of the pipette on the inside wall of the cylinder, close to the top. Dispense the solution slowly and rotate the cylinder with your other hand. You will see the syrup-like solution smear downwards towards the homogenate. Seal cylinder with parafilm and mix very gently by inversion 10-20 times. Rotate the cylinder every time you mix it

steps.title: Aliquot the homogenate into six 50...
steps.instruction: Aliquot the homogenate into six 50 mL Falcon tubes You can also use Nalgene centrifuge tubes. And instead of the SLA-600 TC rotor you will need the SS34

steps.title: Spin down at 588 rpm (57...
steps.instruction: Spin down at 588 rpm (57 g) using the SLA-600 TC rotor at 4-10°C, for 2 min only. A tiny pellet might be visible. If no pellet is observed, continue with next step without changing tubes. If using the SS34, adjust the speed to correspond to the same g values used on the SLA-600 TC 00:02:00, Low speed centrifugation

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new set of 50 mL Falcon tubes and spin down at 3308 rpm (1800 g), same rotor, 10 °C, 15 min 00:15:00, Nuclei pellet centrifugation Undetermined, Centrifugation temperature The centrifugal force depends on the genome size but also on the medium's density. The osmoticum used in this buffer (mannitol) is at 0.5 M and seems to work well with genomes upto 3 Gb (N. benthamiana), at 1800 x g, but please see recommendations here . Other cellular material such as starch might contribue to the easy sedimentation of large genomes such as the N. benthamiana.

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a waste container for proper disposal later
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a waste container for proper disposal later. Check the waste disposal regulations for b-mercaptoethanol solutions and solid waste

steps.title: Resuspend each pellet with 50 mL NEB –no β-mercaptoethanol
steps.instruction: Resuspend each pellet with 50 mL NEB –no β-mercaptoethanol. Mix gently by inversion until the pellet is resuspended. Spin down again as before. go to step #10

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to the waste container...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to the waste container for proper disposal This supernatant does not contain β-mercaptoethanol and could be poured down the drain unless you are working with regulated biological material

steps.title: Resuspend each pellet with 5 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend each pellet with 5 mL NEB –no β-mercaptoethanol and collect all pellets into one Falcon tube previously weighted. Add more NEB –no β-mercaptoethanol to final volume of 50 mL

steps.title: Weight the tube to find out...
steps.instruction: Weight the tube to find out the total amount of nuclei isolated. From 20 g of kiwifruit fully developed leaves expect about 0.2 g of nuclei, but this will vary depending on the species and genotype. Keep the tube on ice If you can’t continue with the nuclear genomic DNA extraction, store the nuclei pellet at -80°C until ready to extract the DNA

steps.title: Collect the nuclei by centrifugation go to step #10
steps.instruction: Collect the nuclei by centrifugation go to step #10

steps.title: Resuspend 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend 0.2 g of nuclei pellet in 14 mL lysis buffer, mix by inversion, do not vortex A modified version of the method published by Lopez-Gomez and Gomez-Lim (1992) HortScience 27(5):440-442. This protocol is suitable for plants with a high carbohydrate content.

steps.title: Add 7 μL of RNase A 100 mg/mL
steps.instruction: Add 7 μL of RNase A 100 mg/mL. Mix by inversion

steps.title: Incubate 10 min at 37°C with...
steps.instruction: Incubate 10 min at 37°C with gentle shaking Undetermined, RNA digestion 00:10:00, Lysis and RNA digestion

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.4 mL 5 M K-Acetate pH 7, mix by inversion

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 mL 100% Ethanol, vortex at maximum speed for 30 s

steps.title: Extract the sample with an equal...
steps.instruction: Extract the sample with an equal volume of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1). Mix by inversion 10 times, then place the tube horizontally in orbital shaker at room temperature and shake for 5 min, gently 00:05:00, Chloroform extraction Undetermined, Chlorform extraction

steps.title: Spin down at 3000 rpm 10...
steps.instruction: Spin down at 3000 rpm 10 min at room temperature using a swing bucket rotor 00:10:00, Chloroform extraction centrifugaion

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to a new 50 mL Falcon tube. Add an equal volume of ice cold isopropanol. Mix by inversion and store at -20°C for 30 min 00:30:00, Isopropanol precipitation Undetermined, Isopropanol precipitation If the aqueous phase is not clear, repeat the chloroform extraction (steps 23-24)

steps.title: Spin down at 3000 rpm for...
steps.instruction: Spin down at 3000 rpm for 25 min at room temperature or 10°C 00:25:00, DNA precipitation centrifugation

steps.title: Discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant. Remove all traces of ethanol with a kimwipe without touching the pellet. Air dry the DNA pellet at room temperature for 20-30 min and add 100-200 μL TE buffer pH 7.5. Tap the bottom of the tube to detach the pellet and let it diffuse slowly at room temperature for a few hours or in the refrigerator overnight. Avoid vortexing the DNA solution at high speed. If you are concerned the DNA will bind to the tube irreversible, add the TE buffer immediately and leave the tube open, at room temperature. Any traces of ethanol will evaporte from the solution.

steps.title: Store the DNA at 4°C
steps.instruction: Store the DNA at 4°C. For long term storage, make numbered aliquots and store at -20°C. Use DNA aliquots sequentially and avoid freeze/thaw cycles

steps.title: Discard supernatant and add same volume...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and add same volume of 70% ethanol to wash the pellet. Let it stand at room temperature for ~ 10 min 00:10:00, Elute salts from DNA pellet Undetermined, Elute salts from DNA pellet

steps.title: Collect DNA by centrifugation go to step #26
steps.instruction: Collect DNA by centrifugation go to step #26

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 mL of CTAB/NaCl extraction buffer and 80 μL Proteinase K to the nuclei pellet and vortex gently to resuspend

steps.title: Check the quality of the ngDNA...
steps.instruction: Check the quality of the ngDNA by absorbance ratios at 260/280 nm and 260/230 nm. Aim for ~1.8 and >2.0, respectively.

steps.title: Incubate at 56°C for 1-2 hour...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56°C for 1-2 hour without shaking or until all solution is clear

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 mL of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1) and vortex until an emulsion is obtained

steps.title: Separate the phases by centrifugation using...
steps.instruction: Separate the phases by centrifugation using a swing bucket rotor, 3000 rpm, at room temperature for 15 min 00:15:00, Chloroform extraction centrifugation

steps.title: If the interphase between the organic...
steps.instruction: If the interphase between the organic and aqueous solutions is not tight, you could centrigue the sample for another 15 min or transfer the aqueous phase to a new tube and add equal volume of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol go to step #34 go to step #35

steps.title: Transfer aqueous phase to a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer aqueous phase to a new Falcon tube and add 2 volumes of 100% ethanol. Mix gently. Incubate at -20°C for at least 2 h 02:00:00, Etahnol precipitation Undetermined, Ethanol precipitation

steps.title: Quantify the ngDNA by fluorescence (Qubit...
steps.instruction: Quantify the ngDNA by fluorescence (Qubit or DeNovix) using a high sensitivity kit. Make 10-fold serial dilutions and quantify 1-5 μL

steps.title: Run 100- 200 ng of ngDNA...
steps.instruction: Run 100- 200 ng of ngDNA in a standard 1% agarose gel, stained with Sybr safe. Use lambda DNA (48.5 kbp) as control. If you have access to a pulse field gel electrophoresis system, run ~ 200 ng against a lambda ladder, intact lambda DNA and 1 kb+ ladder.

steps.title: Add 10 μL RNase A 100...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL RNase A 100 mg/mL, mix and incubate at room temperature for 5 min 00:05:00, RNase A digestion

steps.title: Collect the DNA by centrifugation as explained before (Step 26-30)
steps.instruction: Collect the DNA by centrifugation as explained before (Step 26-30)

steps.title: Follow the Qiagen DNease Plant Maxi...
steps.instruction: Follow the Qiagen DNease Plant Maxi kit instructions with the following modifications: The final pellet of nuclei should be divided into 2 tubes and treat each one as the starting material for one extraction using this kit To elute the DNA incubate the binding column with 1 mL of AE buffer for 1 h. Spin down as described in the kit instructions. Add another mL of AE buffer to the binding column and incubate at room temperature for 30 min. Spin down again. Each column/tube will have ~ 2 mL of eluted DNA Combine both samples (~ 4 mL) and add 1/10 volume of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2, mix, and add 2 volumes of 100% ethanol. Mix. Store at 4°C or -20°C ovenight Collect, wash and resuspend the pellet as described in steps 26-30 The fragment size distribution obtained with this kit is 20-40 kbp

steps.title: Depending on the nature of the...
steps.instruction: Depending on the nature of the tissue and the budget, select one of the following methods to isolate the nuclear genomic DNA.
